poster_title: HELLOBENCH: EVALUATING LONG TEXT GENERATION CAPABILITIES OF LARGE LANGUAGE MODELS
authors: Haoran Que, Feiyu Duan, Liqun He, Yutao Mou, Wangchunshu Zhou, Jiaheng Liu, Wenge Rong, Zekun Moore Wang, Jian Yang, Ge Zhang, Junran Peng, Zhaoxiang Zhang, Songyang Zhang, Kai Chen

content: LLMs have shown impressive performance in various NLP tasks.
Existing research focuses on understanding and processing long input text.
Limited focus on long text generation capabilities of LLMs.
Shortfall in comprehensive benchmarks for long text generation.
LLMs struggle to generate text longer than 4000 words.
Open-source LLMs can generate long text but with quality issues.
HelloBench categorizes long text generation into five subtasks.
HelloEval reduces time and effort in human evaluation.
panels.figure_caption: The pipeline of HelloEval has two stages. (top): In the preparation stage, we aim to

content: Most LLMs cannot generate text longer than 4000 words.
HelloEval shows highest correlation with human evaluation.
panels.figure_caption: Scores of different model sizes for open-source LLMs

content: Open-source LLMs can generate long text but with repetition and quality degradation.
HelloEval significantly reduces time and effort in human evaluation.
HelloBench and HelloEval provide valuable tools for evaluating LLMs.
Further research needed to improve long text generation.
